prerequisites: SONiC image built with warm reboot support (most 202005+ images include this)
All critical containers running: database, swss, syncd, bgp, teamd
BGP graceful-restart configured on the device and on BGP peers
Sufficient disk space on /host (>500MB free for dump.rdb checkpoint file)
No stale warmboot files from a previous failed warm reboot
Warm restart feature not currently blocked by an active config-change in progress
ASIC SDK version compatible between current image and next boot image
purpose: Reboot with minimal traffic disruption by preserving forwarding state in ASIC

rollback.action: If warm-reboot script aborted before kexec (pre-shutdown failure), verify the switch is still running normally

commands.command: docker ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}'; show bgp summary; show warm_restart state

rollback.notes: If the warm-reboot script encounters an error before the kexec step, it exits without rebooting. The switch remains operational. Check the abort reason in the script output or: sudo journalctl -u warm-reboot -n 50 --no-pager. Common abort causes: insufficient disk space, missing warm_restart enable, stale checkpoint files. Resolve the issue and retry from step-1.

rollback.action: If warm reboot fell back to cold reboot (reboot-cause shows cold-reboot), check the warm reboot log for the failure stage
rollback.notes: A cold reboot fallback means forwarding state was lost. Wait for full cold boot convergence (2-5 minutes). After containers are up, check: show warm_restart state for stuck entries. Clear any stuck state: redis-cli -n 6 DEL 'WARM_RESTART_TABLE|orchagent'. Do NOT attempt warm reboot again until the root cause is identified.

rollback.action: If post-reboot reconciliation is stuck (orchagent or syncd not reaching 'reconciled' state), force swss restart to trigger cold reconciliation
rollback.notes: This triggers a cold restart of the swss container, which will re-program the ASIC from CONFIG_DB. Traffic will be briefly interrupted during ASIC re-programming (typically 5-30 seconds). After restart, warm_restart state entries will be cleared. Verify with: show warm_restart state; docker exec swss cat /var/log/swss/orchagent.log | tail -20

rollback.action: If checkpoint files are stale or corrupted from a failed warm reboot, clear them before next attempt
rollback.notes: Stale dump.rdb from a failed warm reboot can cause the next warm reboot to restore incorrect state. Always clear checkpoint files after a failed warm reboot. Verify cleared: ls /host/warmboot/ 2>/dev/null && echo FILES_REMAIN || echo CLEARED; redis-cli -n 6 keys 'WARM_RESTART*'

steps.action: Verify platform supports warm reboot and check system readiness

commands.command: show platform summary && show system status

steps.notes: If any critical container is down, resolve that issue first. Warm reboot with unhealthy containers will fall back to cold reboot or abort entirely.

verification.command: show platform summary | grep -iE 'platform|hwsku'; docker ps --filter status=running --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -cE 'database|swss|syncd|bgp|teamd'
verification.confirms_if: System is healthy and all critical containers are in running state
verification.look_for: All five critical containers running (count=5) and platform identified

steps.action: Check for stale warmboot files and state from a previous failed warm reboot
steps.notes: If stale files exist, remove them before proceeding: sudo rm -f /host/warmboot/dump.rdb. If WARM_RESTART_TABLE shows a non-idle state, a previous warm reboot did not complete cleanly — investigate with 'show warm_restart state' before continuing.

verification.command: redis-cli -n 6 HGETALL 'WARM_RESTART_TABLE|system'; ls /host/warmboot/dump.rdb 2>/dev/null && echo STALE_FILE_EXISTS || echo NO_STALE_FILE
verification.confirms_if: No residual state from a previous failed warm reboot
verification.look_for: No stale warmboot files. WARM_RESTART_TABLE|system state should be empty or 'DISABLED'.

steps.action: Enable warm restart if not already enabled

daemon_impact.impact: Will use warm restart reconciliation path on next boot
Will initialize in warm mode on next boot, skipping ASIC full reset

steps.notes: If not enabled, run: config warm_restart enable system. To enable per-daemon: config warm_restart enable swss. Individual daemon timers can be set with: config warm_restart timer bgp <seconds>. Default BGP timer is 300s.

verification.confirms_if: Warm restart is enabled at system level

daemon_impact.impact: Will advertise graceful-restart capability to peers; restart time determines route retention window
Maintains RIB during BGP restart window

steps.notes: To enable BGP GR in FRR config: 'bgp graceful-restart' under the router bgp block. The restart timer should exceed the expected warm reboot duration (typically 60-120 seconds). Check peer GR support: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <peer> | grep -i graceful'

verification.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors' | grep -i 'graceful-restart'
verification.confirms_if: BGP GR is active with at least one peer supporting it
verification.look_for: graceful-restart capability advertised and received from peers

steps.warning: If BGP peers do not support graceful-restart, routes will be withdrawn when sessions drop during reboot — this causes a full traffic outage equivalent to cold reboot.

steps.notes: config save writes the in-memory CONFIG_DB to /etc/sonic/config_db.json. This ensures post-reboot config matches the pre-reboot state. Also capture a timestamped backup: sudo sonic-cfggen -d --print-data > /tmp/config_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).json

verification.look_for: File modification timestamp is current (just updated)

steps.action: Verify no pending unsaved config changes and check disk space

commands.command: df -h /host && sudo diff <(redis-cli -n 4 --no-auth-warning SAVE; cat /etc/sonic/config_db.json | python3 -m json.tool) /dev/null 2>&1 | head -5

steps.notes: dump.rdb size scales with route and FDB table sizes. A switch with 100k+ routes may need 500MB or more. Warm reboot will abort if /host runs out of space during checkpoint write.

verification.confirms_if: Sufficient disk space for warmboot checkpoint and config is saved
verification.look_for: At least 500MB free on /host; config_db.json recently modified

daemon_impact.impact: Frozen, then checkpointed. On boot-up, replays APPL_DB entries and reconciles against ASIC_DB
SAI warm shutdown preserves ASIC forwarding state. Warm init on boot-up skips ASIC full reset
Triggers graceful-restart notification to peers; sessions are held by peers for the GR timer duration
RIB preserved in memory; fpmsyncd reconnects post-reboot to re-sync routes
Reconnects to zebra after boot and pushes routes back to APPL_DB for orchagent reconciliation
Performs BGSAVE to /host/warmboot/dump.rdb checkpoint before shutdown

steps.notes: Flags: -v (verbose), -f (force, skip some checks), -s <seconds> (override reboot timeout). The warm-reboot script is at /usr/bin/warm-reboot. The -v flag logs each phase to stdout. After kexec, monitor from the console or poll SSH reconnection. Verify: Script progresses through: freeze orchagent, dump redis checkpoint, SAI warm shutdown, kexec. No ERROR lines in verbose output. (confirms if Switch reboots and comes back up; no pre-shutdown abort errors logged)

steps.action: Post-reboot: verify reboot cause and warm restart state
steps.notes: Run after SSH reconnects (typically 30-90 seconds post-reboot). If reboot-cause shows 'cold-reboot', the warm reboot failed and fell back — check /var/log/warm-reboot.log for the failure reason. Reconciliation may take 1-3 minutes on large route tables.

verification.command: show reboot-cause | head -3; show warm_restart state
verification.confirms_if: Warm reboot completed and all components reconciled successfully
verification.look_for: Reboot cause shows 'warm-reboot'. Warm restart state table shows 'reconciled' for swss, syncd, and bgp

steps.action: Verify all containers are running and orchagent/syncd reconciliation is complete
steps.notes: If orchagent or syncd state is stuck in 'initialized' or 'restored' after 3+ minutes, reconciliation has stalled. Check logs: sudo journalctl -u swss -n 100 --no-pager; docker exec swss cat /var/log/swss/orchagent.log | tail -50

verification.command: redis-cli -n 6 HGET 'WARM_RESTART_TABLE|orchagent' 'state'; redis-cli -n 6 HGET 'WARM_RESTART_TABLE|syncd' 'state'; docker ps --filter status=running --format '{{.Names}}' | wc -l
verification.confirms_if: Full reconciliation completed; ASIC state is consistent with CONFIG_DB
verification.look_for: orchagent state = 'reconciled', syncd state = 'reconciled', all expected containers running

steps.action: Verify BGP sessions re-established and routes preserved in ASIC

commands.command: show bgp summary; show ip route summary; show interfaces status

steps.notes: Compare counts against pre-reboot baseline captured before step-7. If BGP session count is lower, check: 'show bgp neighbors <peer>' for GR negotiation. If routes are missing, check fpmsyncd: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd. ASIC route count: redis-cli -n 1 HLEN ASIC_DB may differ until orchagent completes full reconciliation.

verification.command: show bgp summary | grep -c Established; show ip route summary | grep -E 'Total|Connected|BGP'; redis-cli -n 1 HLEN 'ROUTE_TABLE:default'
verification.confirms_if: Control plane fully restored, traffic forwarding at pre-reboot parity
verification.look_for: BGP established session count matches pre-reboot baseline; route count in APPL_DB matches pre-reboot; all interfaces in expected state

warnings: Warm reboot preserves forwarding state but traffic may still be impacted for 1-10 seconds depending on BGP GR convergence.
If warm reboot fails mid-sequence, the switch falls back to a cold reboot — full traffic outage will occur.
Do NOT modify CONFIG_DB while warm reboot is in progress; it will corrupt the checkpoint.
BGP peers must support graceful-restart; otherwise sessions will drop and routes will be withdrawn.
Warm reboot does NOT tolerate ASIC SDK incompatibilities between images — verify SDK versions match before cross-version reboot.
LAG/LACP partners must tolerate the brief hello gap during reboot or the LAG will go down.
Some platform SAI implementations do not support warm reboot — check vendor documentation.
